verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam autem audivit verba filiorum Laban dicentium : Tulit Jacob omnia quæ fuerunt patris nostri, et de illius facultate ditatus, factus est inclytus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֗ע אֶת־דִּבְרֵ֤י בְנֵֽי־לָבָן֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לָקַ֣ח יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֵ֖ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְאָבִ֑ינוּ וּמֵאֲשֶׁ֣ר לְאָבִ֔ינוּ עָשָׂ֕ה אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַכָּבֹ֖ד הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But after that he had heard the words of the sons of Laban, saying: Jacob hath taken away all that was our father's, and being enriched by his substance is become great.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen eteensä tuli Labanin lasten puhe, että he sanoivat: Jakob on saattanut itsellensä kaiken meidän isämme tavaran: ja siitä meidän isämme hyvyydestä on hän saattanut itsellensä kaiken tämän tavaran.
verses.king_james: And he heard the words of Laban’s sons, saying, Jacob hath taken away all that was our father’s; and of that which was our father’s hath he gotten all this glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: animadvertit quoque faciem Laban, quod non esset erga se sicut heri et nudiustertius,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥רְא יַעֲקֹ֖ב אֶת־פְּנֵ֣י לָבָ֑ן וְהִנֵּ֥ה אֵינֶ֛נּוּ עִמּ֖וֹ כִּתְמ֥וֹל שִׁלְשֽׁוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And perceiving also, that Laban's countenance was not towards him as yesterday and the other day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob näki Labanin kasvon: ja katso, ei ollut se häntä kohtaan, niinkuin eilen ja entispäivänä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob beheld the countenance of Laban, and, behold, it was not toward him as before.

verses.latin_vulgate: maxime dicente sibi Domino : Revertere in terram patrum tuorum, et ad generationem tuam, eroque tecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶֽל־יַעֲקֹ֔ב שׁ֛וּב אֶל־אֶ֥רֶץ אֲבוֹתֶ֖יךָ וּלְמוֹלַדְתֶּ֑ךָ וְאֶֽהְיֶ֖ה עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Especially the Lord saying to him: Return into the land of thy fathers and to thy kindred, and I will be with thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Jakobille: palaja sinun isäis maalle, ja sukuis tykö: ja minä olen sinun kanssas.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit, et vocavit Rachel et Liam in agrum, ubi pascebat greges,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח יַעֲקֹ֔ב וַיִּקְרָ֖א לְרָחֵ֣ל וּלְלֵאָ֑ה הַשָּׂדֶ֖ה אֶל־צֹאנֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He sent, and called Rachel and Lia into the field, where he fed the flocks,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lähetti Jakob ja kutsui Rakelin ja Lean kedolle laumansa tykö.
verses.king_james: And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field unto his flock,

verses.latin_vulgate: dixitque eis : Video faciem patris vestri quod non sit erga me sicut heri et nudiustertius : Deus autem patris mei fuit mecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֗ן רֹאֶ֤ה אָנֹכִי֙ אֶת־פְּנֵ֣י אֲבִיכֶ֔ן כִּֽי־אֵינֶ֥נּוּ אֵלַ֖י כִּתְמֹ֣ל שִׁלְשֹׁ֑ם וֵֽאלֹהֵ֣י אָבִ֔י הָיָ֖ה עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said to them: I see your father's countenance is not towards me as yesterday and the other day: but the God of my father hath been with me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi heille: minä näen teidän isänne kasvon, ettei hän ole minua kohtaan niinkuin eilen ja entispäivänä, mutta minun isäni Jumala on ollut minun kanssani.
verses.king_james: And said unto them, I see your father’s countenance, that it is not toward me as before; but the God of my father hath been with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipsæ nostis quod totis viribus meis servierim patri vestro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתֵּ֖נָה יְדַעְתֶּ֑ן כִּ֚י בְּכׇל־כֹּחִ֔י עָבַ֖דְתִּי אֶת־אֲבִיכֶֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you know that I have served your father to the uttermost of my power.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja te itse tiedätte, että minä olen kaikella minun väelläni palvellut teidän isäänne.
verses.king_james: And ye know that with all my power I have served your father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et pater vester circumvenit me et mutavit mercedem meam decem vicibus : et tamen non dimisit eum Deus ut noceret mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲבִיכֶן֙ הֵ֣תֶל בִּ֔י וְהֶחֱלִ֥ף אֶת־מַשְׂכֻּרְתִּ֖י עֲשֶׂ֣רֶת מֹנִ֑ים וְלֹֽא־נְתָנ֣וֹ אֱלֹהִ֔ים לְהָרַ֖ע עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Yea your father hath also overreached me, and hath changed my wages ten times: and yet God hath not suffered him to hurt me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta teidän isänne on vietellyt minua, ja muuttanut minun palkkani jo kymmenen kertaa: ei kuitenkaan sallinut Jumala hänen vahinkoa tehdä minulle.
verses.king_james: And your father hath deceived me, and changed my wages ten times; but God suffered him not to hurt me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quando dixit : Variæ erunt mercedes tuæ : pariebant omnes oves varios fœtus ; quando vero e contrario, ait : Alba quæque accipies pro mercede : omnes greges alba pepererunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־כֹּ֣ה יֹאמַ֗ר נְקֻדִּים֙ יִהְיֶ֣ה שְׂכָרֶ֔ךָ וְיָלְד֥וּ כׇל־הַצֹּ֖אן נְקֻדִּ֑ים וְאִם־כֹּ֣ה יֹאמַ֗ר עֲקֻדִּים֙ יִהְיֶ֣ה שְׂכָרֶ֔ךָ וְיָלְד֥וּ כׇל־הַצֹּ֖אן עֲקֻדִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: If at any time, he said: The speckled shall be thy wages: all the sheep brought forth speckled: but when he said on the contrary: Thou shalt take all the white one for thy wages: all the flocks brought forth white ones.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska hän sanoi: kirjavat pitää oleman sinun palkkas, niin kaikki lauma kantoi kirjavia. Mutta jos hän sanoi: pilkulliset pitää oleman sinun palkkas, niin kaikki lauma kantoi pilkullisia.
verses.king_james: If he said thus, The speckled shall be thy wages; then all the cattle bare speckled: and if he said thus, The ringstraked shall be thy hire; then bare all the cattle ringstraked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulitque Deus substantiam patris vestri, et dedit mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּצֵּ֧ל אֱלֹהִ֛ים אֶת־מִקְנֵ֥ה אֲבִיכֶ֖ם וַיִּתֶּן־לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And God hath taken your father's substance, and given it to me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on Jumala ottanut teidän isänne hyvyyden ja antanut minulle.
verses.king_james: Thus God hath taken away the cattle of your father, and given them to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam enim conceptus ovium tempus advenerat, levavi oculos meos, et vidi in somnis ascendentes mares super feminas, varios et maculosos, et diversorum colorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י בְּעֵת֙ יַחֵ֣ם הַצֹּ֔אן וָאֶשָּׂ֥א עֵינַ֛י וָאֵ֖רֶא בַּחֲל֑וֹם וְהִנֵּ֤ה הָֽעַתֻּדִים֙ הָעֹלִ֣ים עַל־הַצֹּ֔אן עֲקֻדִּ֥ים נְקֻדִּ֖ים וּבְרֻדִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: For after the time came of the ewes conceiving, I lifted up my eyes, and saw in my sleep, that the males which leaped upon the females were of divers colours, and spotted, and speckled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui lauman siittämisen ajalla, nostin minä silmäni ja näin unessa: ja katso, kauriit, jotka nousivat laumain päälle, olivat juonteiset, pilkulliset ja kirjavat.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass at the time that the cattle conceived, that I lifted up mine eyes, and saw in a dream, and, behold, the rams which leaped upon the cattle were ringstraked, speckled, and grisled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque angelus Dei ad me in somnis : Jacob ? Et ego respondi : Adsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלַ֜י מַלְאַ֧ךְ הָאֱלֹהִ֛ים בַּחֲל֖וֹם יַֽעֲקֹ֑ב וָאֹמַ֖ר הִנֵּֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the angel of God said to me in my sleep: Jacob. And I answered: Here I am.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jumalan enkeli sanoi minulle unessa: Jakob; ja minä vastasin: tässä minä olen.
verses.king_james: And the angel of God spake unto me in a dream, saying, Jacob: And I said, Here am I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait : Leva oculos tuos, et vide universos masculos ascendentes super feminas, varios, maculosos, atque respersos. Vidi enim omnia quæ fecit tibi Laban.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר שָׂא־נָ֨א עֵינֶ֤יךָ וּרְאֵה֙ כׇּל־הָֽעַתֻּדִים֙ הָעֹלִ֣ים עַל־הַצֹּ֔אן עֲקֻדִּ֥ים נְקֻדִּ֖ים וּבְרֻדִּ֑ים כִּ֣י רָאִ֔יתִי אֵ֛ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לָבָ֖ן עֹ֥שֶׂה לָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Lift up thy eyes, and see that all the males leaping upon the females, are of divers colours, spotted and speckled. For I have seen all that Laban hath done to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi: nosta silmäs, ja katso, kaikki kauriit, jotka nousevat laumain päälle, ovat juonteiset, pilkulliset ja kirjavat: sillä minä olen nähnyt kaikki, mitä Laban sinulle tekee.
verses.king_james: And he said, Lift up now thine eyes, and see, all the rams which leap upon the cattle are ringstraked, speckled, and grisled: for I have seen all that Laban doeth unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum Deus Bethel, ubi unxisti lapidem, et votum vovisti mihi. Nunc ergo surge, et egredere de terra hac, revertens in terram nativitatis tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָנֹכִ֤י הָאֵל֙ בֵּֽית־אֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֨ר מָשַׁ֤חְתָּ שָּׁם֙ מַצֵּבָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֨ר נָדַ֥רְתָּ לִּ֛י שָׁ֖ם נֶ֑דֶר עַתָּ֗ה ק֥וּם צֵא֙ מִן־הָאָ֣רֶץ הַזֹּ֔את וְשׁ֖וּב אֶל־אֶ֥רֶץ מוֹלַדְתֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I am the God of Bethel, where thou didst anoint the stone, and make a vow to me. Now therefore arise, and go out of this land, and return into thy native country.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen Jumala BetElissä, jossa sinä voitelit kiven, ja jossas teit minulle lupauksen: nouse siis nyt ja lähde tältä maalta, ja palaja syntymä-maalles.
verses.king_james: I am the God of Beth–el, where thou anointedst the pillar, and where thou vowedst a vow unto me: now arise, get thee out from this land, and return unto the land of thy kindred.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderuntque Rachel et Lia : Numquid habemus residui quidquam in facultatibus et hæreditate domus patris nostri ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֤עַן רָחֵל֙ וְלֵאָ֔ה וַתֹּאמַ֖רְנָה ל֑וֹ הַע֥וֹד לָ֛נוּ חֵ֥לֶק וְנַחֲלָ֖ה בְּבֵ֥ית אָבִֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Rachel and Lia answered: Have we any thing left among the goods and inheritance of our father's house?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasivat Rakel ja Lea, ja sanoivat hänelle: onko meillä enää osaa, eli perimistä meidän isämme huoneessa?
verses.king_james: And Rachel and Leah answered and said unto him, Is there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our father’s house?

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne quasi alienas reputavit nos, et vendidit, comeditque pretium nostrum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֧וֹא נׇכְרִיּ֛וֹת נֶחְשַׁ֥בְנוּ ל֖וֹ כִּ֣י מְכָרָ֑נוּ וַיֹּ֥אכַל גַּם־אָכ֖וֹל אֶת־כַּסְפֵּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hath he not counted us as strangers, and sold us, and eaten up the price of us?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö hän ole pitänyt meitä muukalaisina? Sillä hän on myynyt meidät, ja peräti syönyt meidän hintamme.
verses.king_james: Are we not counted of him strangers? for he hath sold us, and hath quite devoured also our money.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed Deus tulit opes patris nostri, et eas tradidit nobis, ac filiis nostris : unde omnia quæ præcepit tibi Deus, fac.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י כׇל־הָעֹ֗שֶׁר אֲשֶׁ֨ר הִצִּ֤יל אֱלֹהִים֙ מֵֽאָבִ֔ינוּ לָ֥נוּ ה֖וּא וּלְבָנֵ֑ינוּ וְעַתָּ֗ה כֹּל֩ אֲשֶׁ֨ר אָמַ֧ר אֱלֹהִ֛ים אֵלֶ֖יךָ עֲשֵֽׂה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But God hath taken our father's riches, and delivered them to us, and to our children: wherefore, do all that God hath commanded thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden kaikki hyvyys, jonka Jumala on ottanut meidän isältämme, se tulee oikeudella meille ja meidän lapsillemme: Nyt siis kaikki, mitä Jumala sinulle on käskenyt, se tee.
verses.king_james: For all the riches which God hath taken from our father, that is ours, and our children’s: now then, whatsoever God hath said unto thee, do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexit itaque Jacob, et impositis liberis ac conjugibus suis super camelos, abiit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֖קׇם יַעֲקֹ֑ב וַיִּשָּׂ֛א אֶת־בָּנָ֥יו וְאֶת־נָשָׁ֖יו עַל־הַגְּמַלִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Jacob rose up, and having set his children and wives upon camels, went his way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousi Jakob; ja nosti lapsensa ja emäntänsä kamelein päälle.
verses.king_james: Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels;

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulitque omnem substantiam suam, et greges, et quidquid in Mesopotamia acquisierat, pergens ad Isaac patrem suum in terram Chanaan.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּנְהַ֣ג אֶת־כׇּל־מִקְנֵ֗הוּ וְאֶת־כׇּל־רְכֻשׁוֹ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר רָכָ֔שׁ מִקְנֵה֙ קִנְיָנ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר רָכַ֖שׁ בְּפַדַּ֣ן אֲרָ֑ם לָב֛וֹא אֶל־יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖יו אַ֥רְצָה כְּנָֽעַן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took all his substance, and flocks, and whatsoever he had gotten in Mesopotamia, and went forward to Isaac, his father, to the land of Chanaan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vei pois kaiken karjansa, ja kaiken tavaransa, minkä hän oli pannut kokoon, sen oman karjansa, kun hän oli saanut Mesopotamiassa: mennäksensä isänsä Isaakin tykö Kanaanin maalle.
verses.king_james: And he carried away all his cattle, and all his goods which he had gotten, the cattle of his getting, which he had gotten in Padan–aram, for to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eo tempore ierat Laban ad tondendas oves, et Rachel furata est idola patris sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלָבָ֣ן הָלַ֔ךְ לִגְזֹ֖ז אֶת־צֹאנ֑וֹ וַתִּגְנֹ֣ב רָחֵ֔ל אֶת־הַתְּרָפִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר לְאָבִֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: At that time Laban was gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole away her father's idols.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Laban oli mennyt keritsemään laumaansa. Ja Rakel varasti isänsä epäjumalat.
verses.king_james: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel had stolen the images that were her father’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noluitque Jacob confiteri socero suo quod fugeret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגְנֹ֣ב יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֶת־לֵ֥ב לָבָ֖ן הָאֲרַמִּ֑י עַל־בְּלִי֙ הִגִּ֣יד ל֔וֹ כִּ֥י בֹרֵ֖חַ הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob would not confess to his father-in-law that he was flying away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin varasti Jakob Labanin Syrialaisen sydämen, siinä ettei hän ilmoittanut hänelle, että hän pakeni.
verses.king_james: And Jacob stole away unawares to Laban the Syrian, in that he told him not that he fled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque abiisset tam ipse quam omnia quæ juris sui erant, et amne transmisso pergeret contra montem Galaad,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּבְרַ֥ח הוּא֙ וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁר־ל֔וֹ וַיָּ֖קׇם וַיַּעֲבֹ֣ר אֶת־הַנָּהָ֑ר וַיָּ֥שֶׂם אֶת־פָּנָ֖יו הַ֥ר הַגִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he was gone, together with all that belonged to him, and having passed the river, was going on towards mount Galaad,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän pakeni, ja kaikki, mitä hänellä oli, nousi ja meni virran ylitse; ja meni Gileadin vuorta kohden.
verses.king_james: So he fled with all that he had; and he rose up, and passed over the river, and set his face toward the mount Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: nuntiatum est Laban die tertio quod fugeret Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֻּגַּ֥ד לְלָבָ֖ן בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֑י כִּ֥י בָרַ֖ח יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: It was told Laban on the third day, that Jacob fled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kolmantena päivänä ilmoitettiin Labanille, että Jakob oli paennut.
verses.king_james: And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob was fled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui, assumptis fratribus suis, persecutus est eum diebus septem : et comprehendit eum in monte Galaad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֤ח אֶת־אֶחָיו֙ עִמּ֔וֹ וַיִּרְדֹּ֣ף אַחֲרָ֔יו דֶּ֖רֶךְ שִׁבְעַ֣ת יָמִ֑ים וַיַּדְבֵּ֥ק אֹת֖וֹ בְּהַ֥ר הַגִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days; and overtook him in the mount of Galaad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän otti veljensä kanssansa, ja ajoi häntä takaa seitsemän päiväkunnan matkan: ja saavutti hänen Gileadin vuorella.
verses.king_james: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days’ journey; and they overtook him in the mount Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viditque in somnis dicentem sibi Deum : Cave ne quidquam aspere loquaris contra Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֧א אֱלֹהִ֛ים אֶל־לָבָ֥ן הָאֲרַמִּ֖י בַּחֲלֹ֣ם הַלָּ֑יְלָה וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ הִשָּׁ֧מֶר לְךָ֛ פֶּן־תְּדַבֵּ֥ר עִֽם־יַעֲקֹ֖ב מִטּ֥וֹב עַד־רָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he saw in a dream God, saying to him: Take heed thou speak not any thing harshly against Jacob.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jumala oli tullut Labanin Syrialaisen tykö yöllä unessa ja sanonut hänelle: kavahda sinuas, ettes puhu Jakobille hyvää eli pahaa.
verses.king_james: And God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said unto him, Take heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jamque Jacob extenderat in monte tabernaculum : cumque ille consecutus fuisset eum cum fratribus suis, in eodem monte Galaad fixit tentorium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשֵּׂ֥ג לָבָ֖ן אֶֽת־יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְיַעֲקֹ֗ב תָּקַ֤ע אֶֽת־אׇהֳלוֹ֙ בָּהָ֔ר וְלָבָ֛ן תָּקַ֥ע אֶת־אֶחָ֖יו בְּהַ֥ר הַגִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountain: and when he, with his brethren, had overtaken him, he pitched his tent in the same mount of Galaad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban lähestyi Jakobia. Mutta Jakob oli majansa tehnyt vuorelle. Ja Laban teki veljinensä myös majansa Gileadin vuorelle.
verses.king_james: Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mount: and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mount of Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ad Jacob : Quare ita egisti, ut clam me abigeres filias meas quasi captivas gladio ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָבָן֙ לְיַעֲקֹ֔ב מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֔יתָ וַתִּגְנֹ֖ב אֶת־לְבָבִ֑י וַתְּנַהֵג֙ אֶת־בְּנֹתַ֔י כִּשְׁבֻי֖וֹת חָֽרֶב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Jacob: Why hast thou done thus, to carry away, without my knowledge, my daughters as captives taken with the sword?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Laban Jakobille: mitäs olet tehnyt, ettäs varastit minun sydämeni ja olet vienyt pois minun tyttäreni niinkuin miekalla otetut?
verses.king_james: And Laban said to Jacob, What hast thou done, that thou hast stolen away unawares to me, and carried away my daughters, as captives taken with the sword?

verses.latin_vulgate: cur ignorante me fugere voluisti, nec indicare mihi, ut prosequerer te cum gaudio, et canticis, et tympanis, et citharis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֤מָּה נַחְבֵּ֙אתָ֙ לִבְרֹ֔חַ וַתִּגְנֹ֖ב אֹתִ֑י וְלֹא־הִגַּ֣דְתָּ לִּ֔י וָֽאֲשַׁלֵּחֲךָ֛ בְּשִׂמְחָ֥ה וּבְשִׁרִ֖ים בְּתֹ֥ף וּבְכִנּֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why wouldst thou run away privately, and not acquaint me, that I might have brought thee on the way with joy, and with songs, and with timbrels, and with harps?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja miksis salaa pakenit ja varastit sinus minulta, ja et ilmoittanut minulle? että minä olisin sinua saattanut ilolla, ja lauluilla, ja trumpuilla, ja kanteleilla.
verses.king_james: Wherefore didst thou flee away secretly, and steal away from me; and didst not tell me, that I might have sent thee away with mirth, and with songs, with tabret, and with harp?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non es passus ut oscularer filios meos et filias : stulte operatus es : et nunc quidem
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֣א נְטַשְׁתַּ֔נִי לְנַשֵּׁ֥ק לְבָנַ֖י וְלִבְנֹתָ֑י עַתָּ֖ה הִסְכַּ֥לְתָּֽ עֲשֽׂוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and daughters; thou hast done foolishly; and now indeed,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja et sallinut minun suuta antaa minun pojilleni ja tyttärilleni: tämän sinä olet tyhmästi tehnyt.
verses.king_james: And hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? thou hast now done foolishly in so doing.

verses.latin_vulgate: valet manus mea reddere tibi malum : sed Deus patris vestri heri dixit mihi : Cave ne loquaris contra Jacob quidquam durius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֶשׁ־לְאֵ֣ל יָדִ֔י לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת עִמָּכֶ֖ם רָ֑ע וֵֽאלֹהֵ֨י אֲבִיכֶ֜ם אֶ֣מֶשׁ ׀ אָמַ֧ר אֵלַ֣י לֵאמֹ֗ר הִשָּׁ֧מֶר לְךָ֛ מִדַּבֵּ֥ר עִֽם־יַעֲקֹ֖ב מִטּ֥וֹב עַד־רָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is in my power to return thee evil; but the God of your father said to me yesterday: Take heed thou speak not any thing harshly against Jacob.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minulla olis kyllä voimaa tehdä teille pahaa, mutta teidän isänne Jumala sanoi menneenä yönä minulle: kavahda itses puhumasta Jakobille hyvää eli pahaa.
verses.king_james: It is in the power of my hand to do you hurt: but the God of your father spake unto me yesternight, saying, Take thou heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Esto, ad tuos ire cupiebas, et desiderio erat tibi domus patris tui : cur furatus es deos meos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ הָלֹ֣ךְ הָלַ֔כְתָּ כִּֽי־נִכְסֹ֥ף נִכְסַ֖פְתָּה לְבֵ֣ית אָבִ֑יךָ לָ֥מָּה גָנַ֖בְתָּ אֶת־אֱלֹהָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Suppose thou didst desire to go to thy friends, and hadst a longing after thy father's house: why hast thou stolen away my gods?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nyt ettäs kumminkin menit pois, ja niin suuresti ikävoitsit isäs huoneeseen: miksis varastit minun jumalani.
verses.king_james: And now, though thou wouldest needs be gone, because thou sore longedst after thy father’s house, yet wherefore hast thou stolen my gods?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jacob : Quod inscio te profectus sum, timui ne violenter auferres filias tuas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן יַעֲקֹ֖ב וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לְלָבָ֑ן כִּ֣י יָרֵ֔אתִי כִּ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי פֶּן־תִּגְזֹ֥ל אֶת־בְּנוֹתֶ֖יךָ מֵעִמִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Jacob answered: That I departed unknown to thee, it was for fear lest thou wouldst take away thy daughters by force.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob vastasi, ja sanoi Labanille: minä pelkäsin ja luulin sinun väkivallalla ottavan pois minulta sinun tyttäres.
verses.king_james: And Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid: for I said, Peradventure thou wouldest take by force thy daughters from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod autem furti me arguis : apud quemcumque inveneris deos tuos, necetur coram fratribus nostris : scrutare, quidquid tuorum apud me inveneris, et aufer. Hæc dicens, ignorabat quod Rachel furata esset idola.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֠ם אֲשֶׁ֨ר תִּמְצָ֣א אֶת־אֱלֹהֶ֘יךָ֮ לֹ֣א יִֽחְיֶה֒ נֶ֣גֶד אַחֵ֧ינוּ הַֽכֶּר־לְךָ֛ מָ֥ה עִמָּדִ֖י וְקַֽח־לָ֑ךְ וְלֹֽא־יָדַ֣ע יַעֲקֹ֔ב כִּ֥י רָחֵ֖ל גְּנָבָֽתַם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But, whereas, thou chargest me with theft: with whomsoever thou shalt find thy gods, let him be slain before our brethren. Search, and if thou find any of thy things with me, take them away. Now when he said this, he knew not that Rachel had stolen the idols.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kenenkä tyköä sinä löydät jumalas, ei sen pidä elämän. Tunnustele meidän veljeimme läsnä ollessa, mitä omaas on minun tykönäni, ja ota pois se. Mutta ei Jakob tietänyt Rakelin niitä varastaneen.
verses.king_james: With whomsoever thou findest thy gods, let him not live: before our brethren discern thou what is thine with me, and take it to thee. For Jacob knew not that Rachel had stolen them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressus itaque Laban tabernaculum Jacob, et Liæ, et utriusque famulæ, non invenit. Cumque intrasset tentorium Rachelis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֨א לָבָ֜ן בְּאֹ֥הֶל יַעֲקֹ֣ב ׀ וּבְאֹ֣הֶל לֵאָ֗ה וּבְאֹ֛הֶל שְׁתֵּ֥י הָאֲמָהֹ֖ת וְלֹ֣א מָצָ֑א וַיֵּצֵא֙ מֵאֹ֣הֶל לֵאָ֔ה וַיָּבֹ֖א בְּאֹ֥הֶל רָחֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Laban went into the tent of Jacob, and of Lia, and of both the handmaids, and found them not. And when he was entered into Rachel's tent,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Laban meni Jakobin majaan, ja Lean majaan, ja heidän molempain piikainsa majaan, ja ei löytänyt. Ja läksi Lean majasta, ja tuli Rakelin majaan.
verses.king_james: And Laban went into Jacob’s tent, and into Leah’s tent, and into the two maidservants’ tents; but he found them not. Then went he out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.

verses.latin_vulgate: illa festinans abscondit idola subter stramenta cameli, et sedit desuper : scrutantique omne tentorium, et nihil invenienti,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרָחֵ֞ל לָקְחָ֣ה אֶת־הַתְּרָפִ֗ים וַתְּשִׂמֵ֛ם בְּכַ֥ר הַגָּמָ֖ל וַתֵּ֣שֶׁב עֲלֵיהֶ֑ם וַיְמַשֵּׁ֥שׁ לָבָ֛ן אֶת־כׇּל־הָאֹ֖הֶל וְלֹ֥א מָצָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: She, in haste, hid the idols under the camel's furniture, and sat upon them: and when he had searched all the tent, and found nothing,
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Rakel oli ottanut epäjumalat ja pannut kamelein satulan ala, ja istui siihen päälle; mutta Laban etsi kaiken majan ja ei löytänyt.
verses.king_james: Now Rachel had taken the images, and put them in the camel’s furniture, and sat upon them. And Laban searched all the tent, but found them not.

verses.latin_vulgate: ait : Ne irascatur dominus meus quod coram te assurgere nequeo : quia juxta consuetudinem feminarum nunc accidit mihi : sic delusa sollicitudo quærentis est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל־אָבִ֗יהָ אַל־יִ֙חַר֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔י כִּ֣י ל֤וֹא אוּכַל֙ לָק֣וּם מִפָּנֶ֔יךָ כִּי־דֶ֥רֶךְ נָשִׁ֖ים לִ֑י וַיְחַפֵּ֕שׂ וְלֹ֥א מָצָ֖א אֶת־הַתְּרָפִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: She said: Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise up before thee, because it has now happened to me according to the custom of women. So his careful search was in vain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi isällensä: älköön minun herrani vihastuko, etten minä saa nousta sinun edessäs: sillä minulle tapahtuu vaimoin menon jälkeen: Ja hän etsi, ja ei löytänyt epäjumaliansa.
verses.king_james: And she said to her father, Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise up before thee; for the custom of women is upon me. And he searched, but found not the images.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tumensque Jacob, cum jurgio ait : Quam ob culpam meam, et ob quod peccatum meum sic exarsisti post me,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֥חַר לְיַעֲקֹ֖ב וַיָּ֣רֶב בְּלָבָ֑ן וַיַּ֤עַן יַעֲקֹב֙ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לְלָבָ֔ן מַה־פִּשְׁעִי֙ מַ֣ה חַטָּאתִ֔י כִּ֥י דָלַ֖קְתָּ אַחֲרָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob being angry, said in a chiding manner: For what fault of mine, and for what offence on my part hast thou so hotly pursued me,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob vihastui ja riiteli Labanin kanssa; vastasi ja sanoi hänelle: mitä minä olen rikkonut, ja mikä minun pahatekoni on, ettäs minua olet niin vihaisesti ajanut takaa?
verses.king_james: And Jacob was wroth, and chode with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? what is my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?

verses.latin_vulgate: et scrutatus es omnem supellectilem meam ? quid invenisti de cuncta substantia domus tuæ ? pone hic coram fratribus meis, et fratribus tuis, et judicent inter me et te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־מִשַּׁ֣שְׁתָּ אֶת־כׇּל־כֵּלַ֗י מַה־מָּצָ֙אתָ֙ מִכֹּ֣ל כְּלֵי־בֵיתֶ֔ךָ שִׂ֣ים כֹּ֔ה נֶ֥גֶד אַחַ֖י וְאַחֶ֑יךָ וְיוֹכִ֖יחוּ בֵּ֥ין שְׁנֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And searched all my household stuff? What hast thou found of all the substance of thy house? lay it here before my brethren, and thy brethren, and let them judge between me and thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet etsiskellyt kaikki minun taloni kalut, mitäs olet löytänyt kaikista sinun talois kaluista? Tuo tähän minun ja sinun veljeis eteen, ratkaista meidän kahden vaiheellamme.
verses.king_james: Whereas thou hast searched all my stuff, what hast thou found of all thy household stuff? set it here before my brethren and thy brethren, that they may judge betwixt us both.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco viginti annis fui tecum ? oves tuæ et capræ steriles non fuerunt, arietes gregis tui non comedi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶה֩ עֶשְׂרִ֨ים שָׁנָ֤ה אָנֹכִי֙ עִמָּ֔ךְ רְחֵלֶ֥יךָ וְעִזֶּ֖יךָ לֹ֣א שִׁכֵּ֑לוּ וְאֵילֵ֥י צֹאנְךָ֖ לֹ֥א אָכָֽלְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Have I, therefore, been with thee twenty years? thy ewes and goats were not barren, the rams of thy flocks I did not eat:
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen ollut sinun tykönäs jo kaksikymmentä ajastaikaa, ja sinun lampaas ja vuohes ei ole hedelmättömät olleet: ja en ole minä syönyt oinaita sinun laumastas.
verses.king_james: This twenty years have I been with thee; thy ewes and thy she goats have not cast their young, and the rams of thy flock have I not eaten.

verses.latin_vulgate: nec captum a bestia ostendi tibi, ego damnum omne reddebam : quidquid furto peribat, a me exigebas :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טְרֵפָה֙ לֹא־הֵבֵ֣אתִי אֵלֶ֔יךָ אָנֹכִ֣י אֲחַטֶּ֔נָּה מִיָּדִ֖י תְּבַקְשֶׁ֑נָּה גְּנֻֽבְתִ֣י י֔וֹם וּגְנֻֽבְתִ֖י לָֽיְלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Neither did I shew thee that which the beast had torn; I made good all the damage: whatsoever was lost by theft, thou didst exact it of me:
verses.finnish_biblia: Enkä ole tuonut sinulle pedolta haaskattua, mutta minun piti sen maksaman, sen olet sinä minun kädestäni vaatinut: niin myös mitä ikänänsä sinulta päivällä eli yöllä varastettu oli.
verses.king_james: That which was torn of beasts I brought not unto thee; I bare the loss of it; of my hand didst thou require it, whether stolen by day, or stolen by night.

verses.latin_vulgate: die noctuque æstu urebar, et gelu, fugiebatque somnus ab oculis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָיִ֧יתִי בַיּ֛וֹם אֲכָלַ֥נִי חֹ֖רֶב וְקֶ֣רַח בַּלָּ֑יְלָה וַתִּדַּ֥ד שְׁנָתִ֖י מֵֽעֵינָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Day and night was I parched with heat, and with frost, and sleep departed from my eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Päivällä on minun helle väsyttänyt, ja yöllä vilu: ja uni on paennut minun silmistäni.
verses.king_james: Thus I was; in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep departed from mine eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicque per viginti annos in domo tua servivi tibi, quatuordecim pro filiabus, et sex pro gregibus tuis : immutasti quoque mercedem meam decem vicibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶה־לִּ֞י עֶשְׂרִ֣ים שָׁנָה֮ בְּבֵיתֶ֒ךָ֒ עֲבַדְתִּ֜יךָ אַרְבַּֽע־עֶשְׂרֵ֤ה שָׁנָה֙ בִּשְׁתֵּ֣י בְנֹתֶ֔יךָ וְשֵׁ֥שׁ שָׁנִ֖ים בְּצֹאנֶ֑ךָ וַתַּחֲלֵ֥ף אֶת־מַשְׂכֻּרְתִּ֖י עֲשֶׂ֥רֶת מֹנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in this manner have I served thee in thy house twenty years, fourteen for thy daughters, and six for thy flocks: thou hast changed also my wages ten times.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen jo kaksikymmentä ajastaikaa ollut sinun huoneessas, neljätoistakymmentä ajastaikaa palvelin minä sinua kahden tyttäres tähden, ja kuusi ajastaikaa sinun laumas tähden: ja sinä olet muuttanut minun palkkani kymmenen kertaa.
verses.king_james: Thus have I been twenty years in thy house; I served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy cattle: and thou hast changed my wages ten times.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nisi Deus patris mei Abraham, et timor Isaac affuisset mihi, forsitan modo nudum me dimisisses : afflictionem meam et laborem manuum mearum respexit Deus, et arguit te heri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לוּלֵ֡י אֱלֹהֵ֣י אָבִי֩ אֱלֹהֵ֨י אַבְרָהָ֜ם וּפַ֤חַד יִצְחָק֙ הָ֣יָה לִ֔י כִּ֥י עַתָּ֖ה רֵיקָ֣ם שִׁלַּחְתָּ֑נִי אֶת־עׇנְיִ֞י וְאֶת־יְגִ֧יעַ כַּפַּ֛י רָאָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים וַיּ֥וֹכַח אָֽמֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Unless the God of my father, Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had stood by me, peradventure now thou hadst sent me away naked: God beheld my affliction and the labour of my hands, and rebuked thee yesterday.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jollei minun isäni Jumala, Abrahamin Jumala ja Isaakin pelko olisi ollut minun puolellani; niin sinä peräti tyhjänä olisit minun päästänyt. Mutta Jumala on nähnyt minun vaivani ja kätteni työt, ja nuhteli menneenä yönä sinua.
verses.king_james: Except the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely thou hadst sent me away now empty. God hath seen mine affliction and the labour of my hands, and rebuked thee yesternight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei Laban : Filiæ meæ et filii, et greges tui, et omnia quæ cernis, mea sunt : quid possum facere filiis et nepotibus meis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַן לָבָ֜ן וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֶֽל־יַעֲקֹ֗ב הַבָּנ֨וֹת בְּנֹתַ֜י וְהַבָּנִ֤ים בָּנַי֙ וְהַצֹּ֣אן צֹאנִ֔י וְכֹ֛ל אֲשֶׁר־אַתָּ֥ה רֹאֶ֖ה לִי־ה֑וּא וְלִבְנֹתַ֞י מָֽה־אֶעֱשֶׂ֤ה לָאֵ֙לֶּה֙ הַיּ֔וֹם א֥וֹ לִבְנֵיהֶ֖ן אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָלָֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Laban answered him: The daughters are mine, and the children, and thy flocks, and all things that thou seest are mine: what can I do to my children, and grandchildren?
verses.finnish_biblia: Vastasi Laban, ja sanoi Jakobille: tyttäret ovat minun tyttäreni, ja pojat ovat minun poikani, ja laumat ovat minun laumani, ja kaikki mitä sinä näet ovat minun: mitä minä teen tänäpänä minun tyttärilleni taikka heidän lapsillensa, jotka he ovat synnyttäneet?
verses.king_james: And Laban answered and said unto Jacob, These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and these cattle are my cattle, and all that thou seest is mine: and what can I do this day unto these my daughters, or unto their children which they have born?

verses.latin_vulgate: Veni ergo, et ineamus fœdus, ut sit in testimonium inter me et te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה לְכָ֛ה נִכְרְתָ֥ה בְרִ֖ית אֲנִ֣י וָאָ֑תָּה וְהָיָ֥ה לְעֵ֖ד בֵּינִ֥י וּבֵינֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Come, therefore, let us enter into a league; that it may be for a testimony between me and thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tule siis nyt ja tehkäämme liitto, minä ja sinä; joka pitää oleman todistus minun ja sinun vaiheellas.
verses.king_james: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit itaque Jacob lapidem, et erexit illum in titulum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֥ח יַעֲקֹ֖ב אָ֑בֶן וַיְרִימֶ֖הָ מַצֵּבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a title.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin otti Jakob kiven, ja pani pystyälle muistopatsaaksi.
verses.king_james: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixitque fratribus suis : Afferte lapides. Qui congregantes fecerunt tumulum, comederuntque super eum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֤ב לְאֶחָיו֙ לִקְט֣וּ אֲבָנִ֔ים וַיִּקְח֥וּ אֲבָנִ֖ים וַיַּֽעֲשׂוּ־גָ֑ל וַיֹּ֥אכְלוּ שָׁ֖ם עַל־הַגָּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to his brethren: Bring hither stones. And they, gathering stones together, made a heap, and they ate upon it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob sanoi veljillensä: kootkaat kiviä. Ja he toivat kiviä, ja tekivät roukkion; ja atrioitsivat siinä sen roukkion päällä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob said unto his brethren, Gather stones; and they took stones, and made an heap: and they did eat there upon the heap.

verses.latin_vulgate: quem vocavit Laban Tumulum testis : et Jacob, Acervum testimonii, uterque juxta proprietatem linguæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָא־ל֣וֹ לָבָ֔ן יְגַ֖ר שָׂהֲדוּתָ֑א וְיַֽעֲקֹ֔ב קָ֥רָא ל֖וֹ גַּלְעֵֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Laban called it, The witness heap; and Jacob, The hillock of testimony: each of them according to the propriety of his language.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban kutsui hänen Jegar Sahabuta: mutta Jakob kutsui hänen Gilead.
verses.king_james: And Laban called it Jegar–sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Laban : Tumulus iste erit testis inter me et te hodie, et idcirco appellatum est nomen ejus Galaad, id est, Tumulus testis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֔ן הַגַּ֨ל הַזֶּ֥ה עֵ֛ד בֵּינִ֥י וּבֵינְךָ֖ הַיּ֑וֹם עַל־כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא־שְׁמ֖וֹ גַּלְעֵֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Laban said: This heap shall be a witness between me and thee this day, and therefore the name thereof was called Galaad, that is, The witness heap.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Laban sanoi: tämä roukkio olkoon todistus tänäpänä minun ja sinun vaiheellas; sentähden kutsui hän hänen nimensä Gilead.
verses.king_james: And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and thee this day. Therefore was the name of it called Galeed;

verses.latin_vulgate: Intueatur et judicet Dominus inter nos quando recesserimus a nobis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמִּצְפָּה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַ֔ר יִ֥צֶף יְהֹוָ֖ה בֵּינִ֣י וּבֵינֶ֑ךָ כִּ֥י נִסָּתֵ֖ר אִ֥ישׁ מֵרֵעֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord behold and judge between us, when we shall be gone one from the other.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Mitspa; sillä hän sanoi: Herra olkoon peräänkatsoja sinun ja minun vaiheellani, koska me erkanemme toinen toisestamme:
verses.king_james: And Mizpah; for he said, The Lord watch between me and thee, when we are absent one from another.

verses.latin_vulgate: si afflixeris filias meas, et si introduxeris alias uxores super eas : nullus sermonis nostri testis est absque Deo, qui præsens respicit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תְּעַנֶּ֣ה אֶת־בְּנֹתַ֗י וְאִם־תִּקַּ֤ח נָשִׁים֙ עַל־בְּנֹתַ֔י אֵ֥ין אִ֖ישׁ עִמָּ֑נוּ רְאֵ֕ה אֱלֹהִ֥ים עֵ֖ד בֵּינִ֥י וּבֵינֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If thou afflict my daughters, and if thou bring in other wives over them: none is witness of our speech but God, who is present and beholdeth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos sinä vaivaat minun tyttäriäni, taikka otat muita emäntiä, paitsi minun tyttäriäni. Ei ole yhtään ihmistä (joka todistais) meidän kanssamme, mutta katso, Jumala on todistaja minun ja sinun vaiheellas.
verses.king_james: If thou shalt afflict my daughters, or if thou shalt take other wives beside my daughters, no man is with us; see, God is witness betwixt me and thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque rursus ad Jacob : En tumulus hic, et lapis quem erexi inter me et te,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר לָבָ֖ן לְיַעֲקֹ֑ב הִנֵּ֣ה ׀ הַגַּ֣ל הַזֶּ֗ה וְהִנֵּה֙ הַמַּצֵּבָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָרִ֖יתִי בֵּינִ֥י וּבֵינֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said again to Jacob: Behold this heap, and the stone which I have set up between me and thee,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban sanoi (vielä) Jakobille: katso, tämä on roukkio, ja tämä on muistopatsas, jonka minä panen minun ja sinun vaiheelles.
verses.king_james: And Laban said to Jacob, Behold this heap, and behold this pillar, which I have cast betwixt me and thee;

verses.latin_vulgate: testis erit : tumulus, inquam, iste et lapis sint in testimonium, si aut ego transiero illum pergens ad te, aut tu præterieris, malum mihi cogitans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֚ד הַגַּ֣ל הַזֶּ֔ה וְעֵדָ֖ה הַמַּצֵּבָ֑ה אִם־אָ֗נִי לֹֽא־אֶעֱבֹ֤ר אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ אֶת־הַגַּ֣ל הַזֶּ֔ה וְאִם־אַ֠תָּ֠ה לֹא־תַעֲבֹ֨ר אֵלַ֜י אֶת־הַגַּ֥ל הַזֶּ֛ה וְאֶת־הַמַּצֵּבָ֥ה הַזֹּ֖את לְרָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Shall be a witness: this heap, I say, and the stone, be they for a testimony, if either I shall pass beyond it going towards thee, or thou shalt pass beyond it thinking harm to me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä roukkio olkoon todistus, ja tämä patsas olkoon myös todistus, etten minä käy ylitse tämän roukkion sinun tykös, etkä sinä käy ylitse tämän roukkion ja tämän patsaan minun tyköni, vahinkoa tekemään.
verses.king_james: This heap be witness, and this pillar be witness, that I will not pass over this heap to thee, and that thou shalt not pass over this heap and this pillar unto me, for harm.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus Abraham, et Deus Nachor, judicet inter nos, Deus patris eorum. Juravit ergo Jacob per timorem patris sui Isaac :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהֵ֨י אַבְרָהָ֜ם וֵֽאלֹהֵ֤י נָחוֹר֙ יִשְׁפְּט֣וּ בֵינֵ֔ינוּ אֱלֹהֵ֖י אֲבִיהֶ֑ם וַיִּשָּׁבַ֣ע יַעֲקֹ֔ב בְּפַ֖חַד אָבִ֥יו יִצְחָֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nachor, the God of their father, judge between us. And Jacob swore by the fear of his father Isaac:
verses.finnish_biblia: Abrahamin Jumala ja Nahorin Jumala ja heidän isäinsä Jumala, ratkaiskoon meidän välillämme. Ja Jakob vannoi isänsä Isaakin pelvon kautta.
verses.king_james: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge betwixt us. And Jacob sware by the fear of his father Isaac.

verses.latin_vulgate: immolatisque victimis in monte, vocavit fratres suos ut ederent panem. Qui cum comedissent, manserunt ibi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּזְבַּ֨ח יַעֲקֹ֥ב זֶ֙בַח֙ בָּהָ֔ר וַיִּקְרָ֥א לְאֶחָ֖יו לֶאֱכׇל־לָ֑חֶם וַיֹּ֣אכְלוּ לֶ֔חֶם וַיָּלִ֖ינוּ בָּהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after he had offered sacrifices in the mountain, he called his brethren to eat bread. And when they had eaten, they lodged there:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob uhrasi vuorella, ja kutsui veljensä syömään leipää. Ja kuin he olivat syöneet, olivat he yötä vuorella.
verses.king_james: Then Jacob offered sacrifice upon the mount, and called his brethren to eat bread: and they did eat bread, and tarried all night in the mount.

verses.latin_vulgate: Laban vero de nocte consurgens, osculatus est filios, et filias suas, et benedixit illis : reversusque est in locum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁכֵּ֨ם לָבָ֜ן בַּבֹּ֗קֶר וַיְנַשֵּׁ֧ק לְבָנָ֛יו וְלִבְנוֹתָ֖יו וַיְבָ֣רֶךְ אֶתְהֶ֑ם וַיֵּ֛לֶךְ וַיָּ֥שׇׁב לָבָ֖ן לִמְקֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Laban arose in the night, and kissed his sons and daughters, and blessed them: and returned to his place.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta aamulla varhain nousi Laban, ja suuta antoi poikainsa ja tytärtensä, ja siunasi heitä, ja meni matkaansa, ja palasi kotiansa.
verses.king_james: And early in the morning Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them: and Laban departed, and returned unto his place.
